parameters.text: ⚠️ Invalid Format To save an alias, use: /name [MAC_Address] [Alias] Example: /name AA:BB:CC:11:22 Desktop
⏳ Starting Network Scan... └ ESP32 is probing the subnet.

=🤖 Network Monitor Bot Guide Here are the commands you can use: ⚡️ Core Commands • /wake [MAC/IP/Alias] - Wake up a device • /ping [MAC/IP/Alias] - Check if a device is online • /scan - Trigger a manual network scan 📊 Dashboards & Tracking • /presence - View who is currently home • /list - List devices (add byowner or bylastseen to sort) 📋 Device Management • /name [MAC] [Alias] - Save a short name for a MAC address • /owner [MAC/IP/Alias] [Name] - Assign a device to a person ⚙️ Configuration • /settings network_monitor [key] [value] - Update a system setting (e.g., notify_scan_complete false) 💡 Tip: You can use a saved Alias (like 'MyPC') instead of a MAC/IP for the wake, ping, and owner commands!
=⚠️ Invalid Format Please specify an owner name to assign to this device. Format: /owner [MAC/IP/Alias] [OwnerName] Example: /owner MyPC Bernard
=🆕 New Device Detected! └ MAC: {{ $json.mac }} └ IP: {{ $json.ip }} └ Vendor: {{ $json.vendor || 'Unknown Vendor' }}
=⚠️ The ESP32 is already performing a background scan. Please try again in a moment.
=⚠️ ESP32 Gateway just rebooted! Current WiFi Signal: {{ JSON.parse($json.message).rssi }}dBm
=✅ Scan complete! Found {{ JSON.parse($('On Scan Result').item.json.message).total_found }} devices.
=⚠️ Invalid Settings Format Please provide both a setting key and a value. Format: /settings network_monitor [key] [value] Example: /settings network_monitor notify_scan_complete off
